rationale: Simulation algorithm ontology directly relevant to UOGTO execution and simulation algorithm semantics.
Simulation experiment description standard directly overlaps model/simulation/task/output semantics.
Minimum-information standard for simulation experiments; useful as reporting coverage comparator.
Ontology for simulation, modelling, and optimization is a high-priority comparator for UOGTO execution semantics.
Modelling-oriented upper/reference ontology likely to affect simulation ontology alignment decisions.
Ontology family around computational modelling marketplace semantics; compare service/model/execution coverage.
Structured game concept vocabulary relevant to game specification and outcome comparisons.
Formal game description language resources overlap rules, legal moves, state, terminality, and outcomes.
System dynamics schema standard overlaps dynamic models, stocks, flows, feedback, and simulation execution.
Discrete-event simulation formalism overlaps event, state transition, coupled model, and simulation-time semantics.
Distributed simulation standard likely relevant, but standard access and redistribution terms need review.
Agent-based modelling reporting protocol overlaps model specification, agents, state, scheduling, and processes.
Multi-agent policy ontology may overlap deontic/agent modules, but source artifact and licence need review.
W3C provenance ontology is already aligned in UOGTO and directly overlaps execution provenance.
Plan/execution vocabulary overlaps UOGTO executable workflow and simulation-step semantics.
W3C time ontology supports temporal semantics for events, traces, sessions, and simulation time.
Observation/procedure/result semantics can support simulation output and trace comparisons.
Existing UOGTO alignment target and general metadata/action vocabulary relevant to discoverability and actions.
Foundational ontology comparator for process/object/role/time semantics used by many applied ontologies.
Foundational ontology comparator for events, agents, social objects, qualities, and roles.
Conceptual modelling/foundational ontology resources can inform role, relation, event, and social commitment mappings.
